rationale: Both outputs correctly restore the sequence staffing choice, demand realization, and fulfillment outcome, and both clearly bound unspecified costs, targets, constraints, and unmet-order treatment. B is preferable because it gives the requested diagnosis and replacement directly, with one compact object-to-timing mapping and no visible internal readiness layer. A delays the usable answer with eligibility scaffolding, repeats the same timing and role distinctions across the diagnosis, gate table, replacement, and labeled summary, and adds a too-little-versus-too-much capacity tradeoff even though the objective and consequences of excess capacity were not supplied. That is a limited scope overreach rather than an empirical factual claim or material fidelity defect.
